steps.title: Identify the phrase's role
steps.content: The underlined phrase comes right after the statement that advertisers hesitated to enter television. The word "particularly" signals that the phrase gives a more specific circumstance explaining that hesitation: television-set manufacturing had stalled during World War II.

steps.title: Reject unrelated functions
steps.content: Choice A is about why a type of television programming was popular, but the phrase is about manufacturing delays and advertiser hesitation. Choice B is about comparing radio and television beginnings, but the underlined phrase makes no such comparison. Choice C is about how broadcasters persuaded advertisers, which appears later rather than in the phrase.

steps.title: Name the specific reason
steps.content: Choice D fits because the phrase identifies a concrete reason some advertisers hesitated to support television: with new television sets not being manufactured, the size of the audience was uncertain. The answer is D.
